item.title: Thinking in Uncertainty: Mitigating Hallucinations in MLRMs with Latent Entropy-Aware Decoding
item.description:
By Zhongxing Xu, Zhonghua Wang, Zhe Qian, Dachuan Shi, Feilong Tang, et al.
Recent advancements in multimodal large reasoning models (MLRMs) have significantly improved performance in visual question answering. However, we observe that transition words (e.g., because, however, and wait) are closely associated with hallucinations and tend to exhibit high-entropy states. We a...

item.title: MEMO: Memory-Augmented Model Context Optimization for Robust Multi-Turn Multi-Agent LLM Games
item.description:
By Yunfei Xie, Kevin Wang, Bobby Cheng, Jianzhu Yao, Zhizhou Sha, et al.
Multi-turn, multi-agent LLM game evaluations often exhibit substantial run-to-run variance. In long-horizon interactions, small early deviations compound across turns and are amplified by multi-agent coupling. This biases win rate estimates and makes rankings unreliable across repeated tournaments. ...

item.title: Qianfan-OCR: A Unified End-to-End Model for Document Intelligence
item.description:
By Daxiang Dong, Mingming Zheng, Dong Xu, Chunhua Luo, Bairong Zhuang, et al.
We present Qianfan-OCR, a 4B-parameter end-to-end vision-language model that unifies document parsing, layout analysis, and document understanding within a single architecture. It performs direct image-to-Markdown conversion and supports diverse prompt-driven tasks including table extraction, chart ...

item.title: Sparking Scientific Creativity via LLM-Driven Interdisciplinary Inspiration
item.description:
By Priyanka Kargupta, Shuhaib Mehri, Dilek Hakkani-Tur, Jiawei Han
Despite interdisciplinary research leading to larger and longer-term impact, most work remains confined to single-domain academic silos. Recent AI-based approaches to scientific discovery show promise for interdisciplinary research, but many prioritize rapidly designing experiments and solutions, by...

item.title: Test-Time Strategies for More Efficient and Accurate Agentic RAG
item.description:
By Brian Zhang, Deepti Guntur, Zhiyang Zuo, Abhinav Sharma, Shreyas Chaudhari, et al.
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems face challenges with complex, multihop questions, and agentic frameworks such as Search-R1 (Jin et al., 2025), which operates iteratively, have been proposed to address these complexities. However, such approaches can introduce inefficiencies, including r...

item.title: I Know What I Don't Know: Latent Posterior Factor Models for Multi-Evidence Probabilistic Reasoning
item.description:
By Aliyu Agboola Alege
Real-world decision-making, from tax compliance assessment to medical diagnosis, requires aggregating multiple noisy and potentially contradictory evidence sources. Existing approaches either lack explicit uncertainty quantification (neural aggregation methods) or rely on manually engineered discret...

item.title: ESPIRE: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Embodied Spatial Reasoning of Vision-Language Models
item.description:
By Yanpeng Zhao, Wentao Ding, Hongtao Li, Baoxiong Jia, Zilong Zheng
A recent trend in vision-language models (VLMs) has been to enhance their spatial cognition for embodied domains. Despite progress, existing evaluations have been limited both in paradigm and in coverage, hindering rapid, iterative model development. To address these limitations, we propose ESPIRE, ...

item.title: Theoretical Foundations of Latent Posterior Factors: Formal Guarantees for Multi-Evidence Reasoning
item.description:
By Aliyu Agboola Alege
We present a complete theoretical characterization of Latent Posterior Factors (LPF), a principled framework for aggregating multiple heterogeneous evidence items in probabilistic prediction tasks. Multi-evidence reasoning arises pervasively in high-stakes domains including healthcare diagnosis, fin...

item.title: BERTology of Molecular Property Prediction
item.description:
By Mohammad Mostafanejad, Paul Saxe, T. Daniel Crawford
Chemical language models (CLMs) have emerged as promising competitors to popular classical machine learning models for molecular property prediction (MPP) tasks. However, an increasing number of studies have reported inconsistent and contradictory results for the performance of CLMs across various M...

item.title: GradMem: Learning to Write Context into Memory with Test-Time Gradient Descent
item.description:
By Yuri Kuratov, Matvey Kairov, Aydar Bulatov, Ivan Rodkin, Mikhail Burtsev
Many large language model applications require conditioning on long contexts. Transformers typically support this by storing a large per-layer KV-cache of past activations, which incurs substantial memory overhead. A desirable alternative is ompressive memory: read a context once, store it in a comp...

item.title: SK-Adapter: Skeleton-Based Structural Control for Native 3D Generation
item.description:
By Anbang Wang, Yuzhuo Ao, Shangzhe Wu, Chi-Keung Tang
Native 3D generative models have achieved remarkable fidelity and speed, yet they suffer from a critical limitation: inability to prescribe precise structural articulations, where precise structural control within the native 3D space remains underexplored. This paper proposes SK-Adapter, a simple an...

item.title: ECG-Reasoning-Benchmark: A Benchmark for Evaluating Clinical Reasoning Capabilities in ECG Interpretation
item.description:
By Jungwoo Oh, Hyunseung Chung, Junhee Lee, Min-Gyu Kim, Hangyul Yoon, et al.
While Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) show promising performance in automated electrocardiogram interpretation, it remains unclear whether they genuinely perform actual step-by-step reasoning or just rely on superficial visual cues. To investigate this, we introduce ECG-Reasoning-Benchmark,...

item.title: SuperLocalMemory V3: Information-Geometric Foundations for Zero-LLM Enterprise Agent Memory
item.description:
By Varun Pratap Bhardwaj
Persistent memory is a central capability for AI agents, yet the mathematical foundations of memory retrieval, lifecycle management, and consistency remain unexplored. Current systems employ cosine similarity for retrieval, heuristic decay for salience, and provide no formal contradiction detection....

item.title: Chain-of-Trajectories: Unlocking the Intrinsic Generative Optimality of Diffusion Models via Graph-Theoretic Planning
item.description:
By Ping Chen, Xiang Liu, Xingpeng Zhang, Fei Shen, Xun Gong, et al.
Diffusion models operate in a reflexive System 1 mode, constrained by a fixed, content-agnostic sampling schedule. This rigidity arises from the curse of state dimensionality, where the combinatorial explosion of possible states in the high-dimensional noise manifold renders explicit trajectory plan...

item.title: VAREX: A Benchmark for Multi-Modal Structured Extraction from Documents
item.description:
By Udi Barzelay, Ophir Azulai, Inbar Shapira, Idan Friedman, Foad Abo Dahood, et al.
We introduce VAREX (VARied-schema EXtraction), a benchmark for evaluating multimodal foundation models on structured data extraction from government forms. VAREX employs a Reverse Annotation pipeline that programmatically fills PDF templates with synthetic values, producing deterministic ground trut...

item.title: WiT: Waypoint Diffusion Transformers via Trajectory Conflict Navigation
item.description:
By Hainuo Wang, Mingjia Li, Xiaojie Guo
While recent Flow Matching models avoid the reconstruction bottlenecks of latent autoencoders by operating directly in pixel space, the lack of semantic continuity in the pixel manifold severely intertwines optimal transport paths. This induces severe trajectory conflicts near intersections, yieldin...

item.title: SWE-Skills-Bench: Do Agent Skills Actually Help in Real-World Software Engineering?
item.description:
By Tingxu Han, Yi Zhang, Wei Song, Chunrong Fang, Zhenyu Chen, et al.
Agent skills, structured procedural knowledge packages injected at inference time, are increasingly used to augment LLM agents on software engineering tasks. However, their real utility in end-to-end development settings remains unclear. We present SWE-Skills-Bench, the first requirement-driven benc...

item.title: From Passive Observer to Active Critic: Reinforcement Learning Elicits Process Reasoning for Robotic Manipulation
item.description:
By Yibin Liu, Yaxing Lyu, Daqi Gao, Zhixuan Liang, Weiliang Tang, et al.
Accurate process supervision remains a critical challenge for long-horizon robotic manipulation. A primary bottleneck is that current video MLLMs, trained primarily under a Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) paradigm, function as passive "Observers" that recognize ongoing events rather than evaluating the...

item.title: Semi-Autonomous Formalization of the Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau Equilibrium
item.description:
By Vasily Ilin
We present a complete Lean 4 formalization of the equilibrium characterization in the Vlasov-Maxwell-Landau (VML) system, which describes the motion of charged plasma. The project demonstrates the full AI-assisted mathematical research loop: an AI reasoning model (Gemini DeepThink) generated the pro...

item.title: Residual Stream Duality in Modern Transformer Architectures
item.description:
By Yifan Zhang
Recent work has made clear that the residual pathway is not mere optimization plumbing; it is part of the model's representational machinery. We agree, but argue that the cleanest way to organize this design space is through a two-axis view of the Transformer. A decoder evolves information along two...

item.title: ARISE: Agent Reasoning with Intrinsic Skill Evolution in Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
By Yu Li, Rui Miao, Zhengling Qi, Tian Lan
The dominant paradigm for improving mathematical reasoning in language models relies on Reinforcement Learning with verifiable rewards. Yet existing methods treat each problem instance in isolation without leveraging the reusable strategies that emerge and accumulate during training. To this end, we...

item.title: ViT-AdaLA: Adapting Vision Transformers with Linear Attention
item.description:
By Yifan Li, Seunghyun Yoon, Viet Dac Lai, Franck Dernoncourt, Jason Kuen, et al.
Vision Transformers (ViTs) based vision foundation models (VFMs) have achieved remarkable performance across diverse vision tasks, but suffer from quadratic complexity that limits scalability to long sequences. Existing linear attention approaches for ViTs are typically trained from scratch, requiri...

item.title: MDM-Prime-v2: Binary Encoding and Index Shuffling Enable Compute-optimal Scaling of Diffusion Language Models
item.description:
By Chen-Hao Chao, Wei-Fang Sun, Junwei Qua, Chun-Yi Lee, Rahul G. Krishnan
Masked diffusion models (MDM) exhibit superior generalization when learned using a Partial masking scheme (Prime). This approach converts tokens into sub-tokens and models the diffusion process at the sub-token level. We identify two limitations of the MDM-Prime framework. First, we lack tools to gu...

item.title: Rethinking UMM Visual Generation: Masked Modeling for Efficient Image-Only Pre-training
item.description:
By Peng Sun, Jun Xie, Tao Lin
Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs) are often constrained by the pre-training of their visual generation components, which typically relies on inefficient paradigms and scarce, high-quality text-image paired data. In this paper, we systematically analyze pre-training recipes for UMM visual generation a...

item.title: Omnilingual MT: Machine Translation for 1,600 Languages
item.description:
By Omnilingual MT Team, Belen Alastruey, Niyati Bafna, Andrea Caciolai, Kevin Heffernan, et al.
High-quality machine translation (MT) can scale to hundreds of languages, setting a high bar for multilingual systems. However, compared to the world's 7,000 languages, current systems still offer only limited coverage: about 200 languages on the target side, and maybe a few hundreds more on the sou...

item.title: AdaMem: Adaptive User-Centric Memory for Long-Horizon Dialogue Agents
item.description:
By Shannan Yan, Jingchen Ni, Leqi Zheng, Jiajun Zhang, Peixi Wu, et al.
Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly rely on external memory to support long-horizon interaction, personalized assistance, and multi-step reasoning. However, existing memory systems still face three core challenges: they often rely too heavily on semantic similarity, which can miss evidenc...

item.title: Conservative Offline Robot Policy Learning via Posterior-Transition Reweighting
item.description:
By Wanpeng Zhang, Hao Luo, Sipeng Zheng, Yicheng Feng, Haiweng Xu, et al.
Offline post-training adapts a pretrained robot policy to a target dataset by supervised regression on recorded actions. In practice, robot datasets are heterogeneous: they mix embodiments, camera setups, and demonstrations of varying quality, so many trajectories reflect recovery behavior, inconsis...

item.title: BenchPreS: A Benchmark for Context-Aware Personalized Preference Selectivity of Persistent-Memory LLMs
item.description:
By Sangyeon Yoon, Sunkyoung Kim, Hyesoo Hong, Wonje Jeung, Yongil Kim, et al.
Large language models (LLMs) increasingly store user preferences in persistent memory to support personalization across interactions. However, in third-party communication settings governed by social and institutional norms, some user preferences may be inappropriate to apply. We introduce BenchPreS...

item.title: HistoAtlas: A Pan-Cancer Morphology Atlas Linking Histomics to Molecular Programs and Clinical Outcomes
item.description:
By Pierre-Antoine Bannier
We present HistoAtlas, a pan-cancer computational atlas that extracts 38 interpretable histomic features from 6,745 diagnostic H&E slides across 21 TCGA cancer types and systematically links every feature to survival, gene expression, somatic mutations, and immune subtypes. All associations are cova...

item.title: When AI Navigates the Fog of War
item.description:
By Ming Li, Xirui Li, Tianyi Zhou
Can AI reason about a war before its trajectory becomes historically obvious? Analyzing this capability is difficult because retrospective geopolitical prediction is heavily confounded by training-data leakage. We address this challenge through a temporally grounded case study of the early stages of...

item.title: Mixture of Style Experts for Diverse Image Stylization
item.description:
By Shihao Zhu, Ziheng Ouyang, Yijia Kang, Qilong Wang, Mi Zhou, et al.
Diffusion-based stylization has advanced significantly, yet existing methods are limited to color-driven transformations, neglecting complex semantics and material details.We introduce StyleExpert, a semantic-aware framework based on the Mixture of Experts (MoE). Our framework employs a unified styl...

item.title: Kinema4D: Kinematic 4D World Modeling for Spatiotemporal Embodied Simulation
item.description:
By Mutian Xu, Tianbao Zhang, Tianqi Liu, Zhaoxi Chen, Xiaoguang Han, et al.
Simulating robot-world interactions is a cornerstone of Embodied AI. Recently, a few works have shown promise in leveraging video generations to transcend the rigid visual/physical constraints of traditional simulators. However, they primarily operate in 2D space or are guided by static environmenta...

item.title: Anticipatory Planning for Multimodal AI Agents
item.description:
By Yongyuan Liang, Shijie Zhou, Yu Gu, Hao Tan, Gang Wu, et al.
Recent advances in multimodal agents have improved computer-use interaction and tool-usage, yet most existing systems remain reactive, optimizing actions in isolation without reasoning about future states or long-term goals. This limits planning coherence and prevents agents from reliably solving hi...

item.title: InCoder-32B: Code Foundation Model for Industrial Scenarios
item.description:
By Jian Yang, Wei Zhang, Jiajun Wu, Junhang Cheng, Shawn Guo, et al.
Recent code large language models have achieved remarkable progress on general programming tasks. Nevertheless, their performance degrades significantly in industrial scenarios that require reasoning about hardware semantics, specialized language constructs, and strict resource constraints. To addre...

item.title: V-Co: A Closer Look at Visual Representation Alignment via Co-Denoising
item.description:
By Han Lin, Xichen Pan, Zun Wang, Yue Zhang, Chu Wang, et al.
Pixel-space diffusion has recently re-emerged as a strong alternative to latent diffusion, enabling high-quality generation without pretrained autoencoders. However, standard pixel-space diffusion models receive relatively weak semantic supervision and are not explicitly designed to capture high-lev...

item.title: SparkVSR: Interactive Video Super-Resolution via Sparse Keyframe Propagation
item.description:
By Jiongze Yu, Xiangbo Gao, Pooja Verlani, Akshay Gadde, Yilin Wang, et al.
Video Super-Resolution (VSR) aims to restore high-quality video frames from low-resolution (LR) estimates, yet most existing VSR approaches behave like black boxes at inference time: users cannot reliably correct unexpected artifacts, but instead can only accept whatever the model produces. In this ...

item.title: PRISM: Demystifying Retention and Interaction in Mid-Training
item.description:
By Bharat Runwal, Ashish Agrawal, Anurag Roy, Rameswar Panda
We present PRISM, a comprehensive empirical study of mid-training design choices for large language models. Through controlled experiments across seven base models spanning four families (Granite, LLaMA, Mistral, Nemotron-H), two architecture types (dense Transformer and attention-Mamba hybrid), and...

item.title: MosaicMem: Hybrid Spatial Memory for Controllable Video World Models
item.description:
By Wei Yu, Runjia Qian, Yumeng Li, Liquan Wang, Songheng Yin, et al.
Video diffusion models are moving beyond short, plausible clips toward world simulators that must remain consistent under camera motion, revisits, and intervention. Yet spatial memory remains a key bottleneck: explicit 3D structures can improve reprojection-based consistency but struggle to depict m...

item.title: MetaClaw: Just Talk -- An Agent That Meta-Learns and Evolves in the Wild
item.description:
By Peng Xia, Jianwen Chen, Xinyu Yang, Haoqin Tu, Jiaqi Liu, et al.
Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly used for complex tasks, yet deployed agents often remain static, failing to adapt as user needs evolve. This creates a tension between the need for continuous service and the necessity of updating capabilities to match shifting task distributions. O...

item.title: AI Scientist via Synthetic Task Scaling
item.description:
By Ziyang Cai, Harkirat Behl
With the advent of AI agents, automatic scientific discovery has become a tenable goal. Many recent works scaffold agentic systems that can perform machine learning research, but don't offer a principled way to train such agents -- and current LLMs often generate plausible-looking but ineffective id...

item.title: Stereo World Model: Camera-Guided Stereo Video Generation
item.description:
By Yang-Tian Sun, Zehuan Huang, Yifan Niu, Lin Ma, Yan-Pei Cao, et al.
We present StereoWorld, a camera-conditioned stereo world model that jointly learns appearance and binocular geometry for end-to-end stereo video generation.Unlike monocular RGB or RGBD approaches, StereoWorld operates exclusively within the RGB modality, while simultaneously grounding geometry dire...

item.title: Efficient Exploration at Scale
item.description:
By Seyed Mohammad Asghari, Chris Chute, Vikranth Dwaracherla, Xiuyuan Lu, Mehdi Jafarnia, et al.
We develop an online learning algorithm that dramatically improves the data efficiency of reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF). Our algorithm incrementally updates reward and language models as choice data is received. The reward model is fit to the choice data, while the language model...

item.title: Temporal Gains, Spatial Costs: Revisiting Video Fine-Tuning in Multimodal Large Language Models
item.description:
By Linghao Zhang, Jungang Li, Yonghua Hei, Sicheng Tao, Song Dai, et al.
Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are typically trained in multiple stages, with video-based supervised fine-tuning (Video-SFT) serving as a key step for improving visual understanding. Yet its effect on the fine-grained evolution of visual capabilities, particularly the balance between spati...

item.title: Complementary Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
By Dilxat Muhtar, Jiashun Liu, Wei Gao, Weixun Wang, Shaopan Xiong, et al.
Reinforcement Learning (RL) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for training LLM-based agents, yet remains limited by low sample efficiency, stemming not only from sparse outcome feedback but also from the agent's inability to leverage prior experience across episodes. While augmenting agents with hi...

item.title: RAMP: Reinforcement Adaptive Mixed Precision Quantization for Efficient On Device LLM Inference
item.description:
By Arpit Singh Gautam, Saurabh Jha
Post training quantization is essential for deploying large language models (LLMs) on resource constrained hardware, yet state of the art methods enforce uniform bit widths across layers, yielding suboptimal accuracy efficiency trade offs. We present RAMP (Reinforcement Adaptive Mixed Precision), an...

item.title: Efficient Training-Free Multi-Token Prediction via Embedding-Space Probing
item.description:
By Raghavv Goel, Mukul Gagrani, Mingu Lee, Chris Lott
Large language models (LLMs) exhibit latent multi-token prediction (MTP) capabilities despite being trained solely for next-token generation. We propose a simple, training-free MTP approach that probes an LLM using on-the-fly mask tokens drawn from its embedding space, enabling parallel prediction o...

item.title: VideoAtlas: Navigating Long-Form Video in Logarithmic Compute
item.description:
By Mohamed Eltahir, Ali Habibullah, Yazan Alshoibi, Lama Ayash, Tanveer Hussain, et al.
Extending language models to video introduces two challenges: representation, where existing methods rely on lossy approximations, and long-context, where caption- or agent-based pipelines collapse video into text and lose visual fidelity. To overcome this, we introduce VideoAtlas, a task-agnostic e...

item.title: LaDe: Unified Multi-Layered Graphic Media Generation and Decomposition
item.description:
By Vlad-Constantin Lungu-Stan, Ionut Mironica, Mariana-Iuliana Georgescu
Media design layer generation enables the creation of fully editable, layered design documents such as posters, flyers, and logos using only natural language prompts. Existing methods either restrict outputs to a fixed number of layers or require each layer to contain only spatially continuous regio...

item.title: LoST: Level of Semantics Tokenization for 3D Shapes
item.description:
By Niladri Shekhar Dutt, Zifan Shi, Paul Guerrero, Chun-Hao Paul Huang, Duygu Ceylan, et al.
Tokenization is a fundamental technique in the generative modeling of various modalities. In particular, it plays a critical role in autoregressive (AR) models, which have recently emerged as a compelling option for 3D generation. However, optimal tokenization of 3D shapes remains an open question. ...

item.title: Unified Spatio-Temporal Token Scoring for Efficient Video VLMs
item.description:
By Jianrui Zhang, Yue Yang, Rohun Tripathi, Winson Han, Ranjay Krishna, et al.
Token pruning is essential for enhancing the computational efficiency of vision-language models (VLMs), particularly for video-based tasks where temporal redundancy is prevalent. Prior approaches typically prune tokens either (1) within the vision transformer (ViT) exclusively for unimodal perceptio...
